title: arundarbor amplexifolia
side_effects: Arundarbor amplexifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems due to its toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. The plant's sap can also cause skin burns and blisters, especially if it comes into contact with open wounds or sensitive skin. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can cause long-term health issues, including liver and kidney damage.
